title: Chloroform-Methanol Protein Extraction with Zymolyase Treatment for Yeast
author: Jennifer Gin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bhptj5nn

steps.title: Thaw cells at Room temperature Note
steps.instruction: Thaw cells at Room temperature Note: If transferring directly from active cultures, omit this step. Adapt as needed for your specific organism and culturing conditions.

steps.title: Transfer 2-4 OD*mLs of cells to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2-4 OD*mLs of cells to 8-Strip PCR tubes (Axygen, Cat.#14-222-251) or a 96-well PCR plate (ThermoFisher, Cat.#AB0600). A strip of PCR tubes filled 30-40% full of cell pellet is approximately 10-20 OD*mLs of cells.

steps.title: Add 80 µL of LC-MS grade Methanol (VWR Scientific, Cat
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL of LC-MS grade Methanol (VWR Scientific, Cat.#BJLC230-2.5). Pipet to resuspend well.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Chloroform (Sigma-Aldrich, Cat
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Chloroform (Sigma-Aldrich, Cat.#34854). Pipet/vortex to mix. Use a fume hood when handling and pipetting chloroform.

steps.title: Add 60 µL of LC-MS grade Water (VWR Scientific, Cat
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL of LC-MS grade Water (VWR Scientific, Cat.#BJLC365-2.5). Pipet/vortex to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Carefully remove the top layer of...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the top layer of solvent (Methanol + Water) by pipetting.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Methanol. Tip
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Methanol. Tip: Break up the protein pellet by piercing it with the pipet tip, and add Methanol to the bottom of the tube or plate.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C, 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C, 2 minutes .

steps.title: Carefully discard solvent (Methanol + Chloroform)
steps.instruction: Carefully discard solvent (Methanol + Chloroform). Discard in an appropriate waste container for halogenated solvents.

steps.title: Air-dry for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Air-dry for 00:05:00 . Tip: Do not dry for longer than 00:45:00 or the pellet will be difficult to resuspend. Dry samples in a fume hood.

steps.title: Resuspend with 60 µL 100 millimolar...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 60 µL 100 millimolar (mM), Ammonium bicarbonate in 20% Methanol . Note: Samples are typically cloudy in this step. After trypsin digestion they will be nearly clear.

steps.title: Store at -20 °C until ready for Protein Quantitation Assay
steps.instruction: Store at -20 °C until ready for Protein Quantitation Assay.

steps.title: Dilute samples 10 fold by adding...
steps.instruction: Dilute samples 10 fold by adding 5 µL, Protein sample, mix well right before transfer to 45 µL, Water in 8-Strip PCR tubes or 96-well plate. Note: The protein concentration can be determined by using several methods that are available in kits. We use the Bio-Rad DC Protein Assay (Bio-rad Laboratories, Cat.#500-0113, Cat.#500-0114) but the Bradford protein quantification assay is also commonly used. The accuracy of most protein concentration measurements can be variable, thus it is important to minimize differences in sample handling and to use replicates when quantifying the amount of protein in a sample.

steps.title: Transfer 2 replicates of each of...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 replicates of each of the following to Corning 96-Well Black Polystyrene Microplate (Fisher Scientific, Cat.#07-200-590): 5 µL, Water (Blank) 5 µL, Pierce Bovine Serum Albumin Standard Pre-Diluted Set (Std) (ThermoFisher, Cat.#23208) 5 µL, Diluted samples, mix well right before adding to plate (Example 1-20) %s %s

steps.title: Add 25 µL, Bio-Rad DC Protein...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL, Bio-Rad DC Protein Assay Reagent A (Bio-rad Laboratories, Cat.#500-0113) and wait 00:05:00 . .

steps.title: Add 200 µL, Bio-Rad DC Protein...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, Bio-Rad DC Protein Assay Reagent B (Bio-rad Laboratories, Cat.#500-0114) and wait 00:10:00 . .

steps.title: Read plate in the microplate reader...
steps.instruction: Read plate in the microplate reader (280 nm) and calculate protein concentrations.

steps.title: Chemicals to prepare
steps.instruction: Chemicals to prepare: • Prepare 100 millimolar (mM), Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP) solution by dissolving 28.7 mg, TCEP in 1 mL, 100mM Ammonium Bicarbonate • Prepare 200 millimolar (mM), Iodoacetamide (IAA) solution by dissolving 36.8 mg, Iodoacetamide in 1 mL, 100mM Ammonium Bicarbonate •Prepare 1 mg/mL, Trypsin by adding 1 mL, 1mM HCl to 1 mg, Trypsin Store TCEP, IAA, and Trypsin in -20C. IAA is light sensitive. Store in amber tube (Fisher Scientific, Cat.#05-402-31).

steps.title: Dilute protein samples to 2
steps.instruction: Dilute protein samples to 2.4 µg/µL in 100 millimolar (mM), Ammonium Bicarbonate (AMBIC) Mix protein well before you dilute it.

steps.title: Mix protein with TCEP, IAA, and...
steps.instruction: Mix protein with TCEP, IAA, and trypsin in 100 millimolar (mM), Ammonium Bicarbonate (AMBIC) . The final concentrations will be 2 µg/µL, protein (in 50 ul total volume) , 5 millimolar (mM), TCEP , 10 millimolar (mM), IAA , and 2 µL, Trypsin (1 mg/ml) (1:50 trypsin:protein ratio). Adjust as needed for your data acquisition protocols. add 41.67 µL, protein (2.4 ug/ul) add 2.5 µL, TCEP (100 mM) add 2.5 µL, IAA (200mM) add 2 µL, Trypsin (1 mg/ml) add 1.33 µL, AMBIC up to 50 µL, total volume If you do not have enough protein, you can set your final protein concentration to 1 ug/ul. add 20.83 µL, protein (2.4 ug/ul) add 2.5 µL, TCEP (100 mM) add 2.5 µL, IAA (200 mM) add 1 µL, Trypsin (1 mg/ml) add 23.17 µL, AMBIC up to 50 µL, total volume

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 04:00:00 - 16:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 04:00:00 - 16:00:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes .

steps.title: Carefully pipet out clear liquid sample...
steps.instruction: Carefully pipet out clear liquid sample into plastic autosampler vials (ThermoFisherScientific, Cat.#03-060-016) or a 96-well plate (BIO-RAD, Cat.#HSP9601).

steps.title: Store at -20 °C until ready for LC-MS/MS analysis
steps.instruction: Store at -20 °C until ready for LC-MS/MS analysis.

steps.title: Buffer to prepare
steps.instruction: Buffer to prepare: • Prepare Zymolyase Buffer by dissolving 1.822 g, Sorbitol and 0.292 g, Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) in 10 mL, Water

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µL, (7.5 U) of Zymolyase to 200 µL, Zymolyase Buffer before resuspending the cells with it.

steps.title: Incubate in 37 °C water bath (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat
steps.instruction: Incubate in 37 °C water bath (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat.#TSGP2S) for 00:10:00 to digest cell walls.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute then remove the supernatent. 0 µL rpm, °C,
